prompt: I need to export all Draw.io diagrams in my CI/CD pipeline as PDFs with custom naming. Not using GitHub Actions.
expected_output: Recommends drawio-export Docker image with output templates for batch processing
assertions: mentions drawio-export (not drawio-export-action)
includes Docker run command
shows output template syntax with {basename} or {ext}
does not suggest GitHub Actions

prompt: I want to automatically export my .drawio files to PNG whenever I push to my GitHub repository.
expected_output: Recommends drawio-export-action with a basic GitHub Actions workflow
assertions: mentions drawio-export-action
includes 'uses: rlespinasse/drawio-export-action@v2'
shows YAML workflow syntax
mentions format option with png

prompt: I'm building a custom Rust application that needs to programmatically export Draw.io diagrams.
expected_output: Recommends drawio-exporter Rust library for integration into custom tools
assertions: mentions drawio-exporter as a Rust library
links to https://github.com/rlespinasse/drawio-exporter
suggests this is for building custom tools or service integration
does not primarily recommend Docker approach

prompt: How do I use the official Draw.io desktop app to export diagrams?
expected_output: Clarifies that this skill covers third-party tools by @rlespinasse, not official Draw.io features
assertions: clarifies these are third-party tools, not official Draw.io
mentions @rlespinasse as the author
does not pretend to cover official Draw.io functionality
may offer to help with the third-party export tools instead

prompt: I need to export my diagrams.
expected_output: Asks clarifying questions before providing a solution
assertions: asks where the user is exporting (GitHub Actions, local, CI/CD)
asks about the user's goal (simple export, batch, custom processing)
does not immediately dump all options
follows the decision flow from the skill

prompt: I just need to quickly convert a single .drawio file to PDF on my local machine using Docker.
expected_output: Recommends docker-drawio-desktop-headless for simple one-off export
assertions: mentions docker-drawio-desktop-headless
includes Docker run command with -x and -f flags
keeps response focused and concise
does not suggest the more complex drawio-export wrapper

prompt: I'm getting permission denied errors when running the Draw.io export Docker container.
expected_output: Provides permission fix with user/group mapping
assertions: includes the -u $(id -u):$(id -g) flag
mentions HOME environment variable workaround
references error handling section
may suggest debug mode with SCRIPT_DEBUG_MODE
